NSCameraUsageDescription.description: Camera is used to update your profile picture and add attachments in messages.

NSFaceIDUsageDescription.info: Access the ability to authenticate with Face ID.
NSFaceIDUsageDescription.description: Enabling Face ID allows quick and secure authentication.

NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription.info: Access location information when app is in the foreground.
NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription.description: Upwork uses location to verify account information for account and platform security, to display more relevant content, and for other related purposes. To continue, choose "Allow"

NSMicrophoneUsageDescription.description: The Microphone is used to record sound for the videos.

NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription.description: This allows you to share files from your Photo Library.

ats_findings.issue: Insecure communication to staging.platform.usw2.upwork is allowed
ats_findings.description: NSExceptionAllowsInsecureHTTPLoads allows insecure HTTP loads to staging.platform.usw2.upwork, or to be able to loosen the server trust evaluation requirements for HTTPS connections to the domain.

ats_findings.issue: NSIncludesSubdomains set to TRUE for staging.platform.usw2.upwork
ats_findings.description: NSIncludesSubdomains applies the ATS exceptions for the given domain to all subdomains as well. For example, the ATS exceptions in the domain exception dictionary apply to staging.platform.usw2.upwork, as well as math.staging.platform.usw2.upwork, history.staging.platform.usw2.upwork, and so on. Otherwise, if the value is NO, the exceptions apply only to staging.platform.usw2.upwork.

ats_findings.issue: NSRequiresCertificateTransparency set to NO for staging.platform.usw2.upwork
ats_findings.description: Certificate Transparency (CT) is a protocol that ATS can use to identify mistakenly or maliciously issued X.509 certificates. Set the value for the NSRequiresCertificateTransparency key to YES to require that for a given domain, server certificates are supported by valid, signed CT timestamps from at least two CT logs trusted by Apple. This key is optional. The default value is NO.

Binary makes use of insecure API(s).detailed_desc:
The binary may contain the following insecure API(s) _chmod
, _fopen
, _memcpy
, _printf
, _sprintf
, _sscanf
, _stat
, _strcpy
, _strlen
, _strncat
, _strncpy
, _vsnprintf
Binary makes use of insecure API(s).cwe: CWE-676: Use of Potentially Dangerous Function

Binary makes use of Logging function.detailed_desc:
The binary may use _NSLog
 function for logging.
Binary makes use of Logging function.cwe: CWE-532: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File

Binary makes use of malloc function.detailed_desc:
The binary may use _malloc
 function instead of calloc

nx.description: The binary has NX bit set. This marks a memory page non-executable making attacker injected shellcode non-executable.

pie.description: The binary is build with -fPIC flag which enables Position independent code. This makes Return Oriented Programming (ROP) attacks much more difficult to execute reliably.

stack_canary.description: This binary has a stack canary value added to the stack so that it will be overwritten by a stack buffer that overflows the return address. This allows detection of overflows by verifying the integrity of the canary before function return.

arc.description: The binary is compiled with Automatic Reference Counting (ARC) flag. ARC is a compiler feature that provides automatic memory management of Objective-C objects and is an exploit mitigation mechanism against memory corruption vulnerabilities.

rpath.description: The binary has Runpath Search Path (@rpath) set. In certain cases an attacker can abuse this feature to run arbitrary executable for code execution and privilege escalation. Remove the compiler option -rpath to remove @rpath.

code_signature.description: This binary has a code signature.

symbol.description: Debug Symbols are available. To strip debugging symbols, set Strip Debug Symbols During Copy to YES, Deployment Postprocessing to YES, and Strip Linked Product to YES in project's build settings.

nx.description: The binary has NX bit set. This marks a memory page non-executable making attacker injected shellcode non-executable.

pie.description: The binary is built without Position Independent Code flag. In order to prevent an attacker from reliably jumping to, for example, a particular exploited function in memory, Address space layout randomization (ASLR) randomly arranges the address space positions of key data areas of a process, including the base of the executable and the positions of the stack,heap and libraries. Use compiler option -fPIC to enable Position Independent Code. Not applicable for dylibs and static libraries.

strings: Manual audio enabled - respecting user's device choice: %s
Manual Audio Enable - Taking control from WebRTC
Accessing Environment<%s>'s value outside of being installed on a View. This will always read the default value and will not update.

firebase_urls.title: App talks to a Firebase database
firebase_urls.description: The app talks to Firebase database at https://lexical-pattern-853.firebaseio.com

appstore_details.title: Upwork
appstore_details.description:
One app. All of Upwork.

Enjoy a smoother, faster, and more flexible experience designed to help you get work done.

Manage your work on the go. Whether you're hiring, freelancing, or doing both, you can post jobs, submit proposals, message, track projects, and manage contracts — all from a single app. This unified experience saves you time and helps you stay focused on what matters most: getting great work done. It’s everything you need, all in one powerful, easy-to-use app, built to support the way you work.

Terms of Use: https://www.upwork.com/legal#terms-of-use

To learn more about opting out of the "sale" or "sharing" of your personal information, please visit our Privacy Center: https://www.upwork.com/legal#privacy-center

logs.status: Fetching Details from App Store: com.upwork.ios.apps.bolt
Searching for secrets in plist files
Extracting URL and Email from IPA
Performing Malware check on extracted domains

high.title: Insecure communication to staging.platform.usw2.upwork is allowed
high.description: NSExceptionAllowsInsecureHTTPLoads allows insecure HTTP loads to staging.platform.usw2.upwork, or to be able to loosen the server trust evaluation requirements for HTTPS connections to the domain.

warning.title: NSRequiresCertificateTransparency set to NO for staging.platform.usw2.upwork
warning.description: Certificate Transparency (CT) is a protocol that ATS can use to identify mistakenly or maliciously issued X.509 certificates. Set the value for the NSRequiresCertificateTransparency key to YES to require that for a given domain, server certificates are supported by valid, signed CT timestamps from at least two CT logs trusted by Apple. This key is optional. The default value is NO.

warning.title: Binary makes use of insecure API(s)
warning.description:
The binary may contain the following insecure API(s) _chmod
, _fopen
, _memcpy
, _printf
, _sprintf
, _sscanf
, _stat
, _strcpy
, _strlen
, _strncat
, _strncpy
, _vsnprintf

warning.title: Binary makes use of malloc function
warning.description:
The binary may use _malloc
 function instead of calloc

warning.title: Application binary has rpath set
warning.description: The binary has Runpath Search Path (@rpath) set. In certain cases an attacker can abuse this feature to run arbitrary executable for code execution and privilege escalation. Remove the compiler option -rpath to remove @rpath.

warning.title: Application binary does not have symbols stripped
warning.description: Debug Symbols are available. To strip debugging symbols, set Strip Debug Symbols During Copy to YES, Deployment Postprocessing to YES, and Strip Linked Product to YES in project's build settings.

info.title: NSIncludesSubdomains set to TRUE for staging.platform.usw2.upwork
info.description: NSIncludesSubdomains applies the ATS exceptions for the given domain to all subdomains as well. For example, the ATS exceptions in the domain exception dictionary apply to staging.platform.usw2.upwork, as well as math.staging.platform.usw2.upwork, history.staging.platform.usw2.upwork, and so on. Otherwise, if the value is NO, the exceptions apply only to staging.platform.usw2.upwork.

info.title: Binary makes use of Logging function
info.description:
The binary may use _NSLog
 function for logging.

info.title: App talks to a Firebase database
info.description: The app talks to Firebase database at https://lexical-pattern-853.firebaseio.com

secure.title: This application has no privacy trackers
secure.description: This application does not include any user or device trackers. Unable to find trackers during static analysis.

hotspot.title: Found 4 critical permission(s)
hotspot.description:
Ensure that these permissions are required by the application.

NSCameraUsageDescription (dangerous): Access the Camera. - Camera is used to update your profile picture and add attachments in messages.

NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription (dangerous): Access location information when app is in the foreground. - Upwork uses location to verify account information for account and platform security, to display more relevant content, and for other related purposes. To continue, choose "Allow"

NSMicrophoneUsageDescription (dangerous): Access microphone. - The Microphone is used to record sound for the videos.

NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription (dangerous): Access the user's photo library. - This allows you to share files from your Photo Library.

hotspot.title: App may communicate to a server (ocsp.apple.com) in OFAC sanctioned country (Hong Kong)
App may communicate to a server (crl.apple.com) in OFAC sanctioned country (Hong Kong)
